steps.title: Find the ratio of E's position on AC
steps.content: Given CE = 2AE and E is on segment AC, we can write: AC = AE + CE = AE + 2AE = 3AE. Therefore: $$\frac{AE}{AC} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Apply similar triangles theorem
steps.content: Since DE ∥ BC, triangle ADE is similar to triangle ABC. The ratio of corresponding sides from vertex A equals: $$\frac{DE}{BC} = \frac{AE}{AC} = \frac{1}{3}$$
